title: Dashboard
recalcStartedDesc: Fitness metrics will update in a moment
noHistory: No data yet — upload activities to start tracking

trainingStatus.title: Koutsi Daily Feedback
trainingStatus.noFeedback: Get daily coaching feedback on your training state, load trends, and upcoming goals.

fitness.description: A 42-day exponentially weighted average of your daily training load. It represents your long-term fitness level — the higher the number, the more training your body has adapted to.

fatigue.description: A 7-day exponentially weighted average of your daily training load. It reflects how much you have trained recently. A high fatigue value means you are currently tired from recent hard training.

form.description: Calculated as fitness − fatigue, using the values from the end of the previous day (your freshness at the start of today, before today's training). Because today's fitness and fatigue already include today's load, the form shown won't exactly equal the fitness and fatigue numbers next to it. A positive value means you are rested relative to your fitness (fresh/peak). A negative value means fatigue outweighs fitness (tired/overreached). Aim for a slightly positive form before a key event.

ftp.description: The highest average power (in watts) you can sustain for approximately one hour. FTP is used to define training zones and to calculate training load for power-based workouts.

load.description: A single number that quantifies the training load of one workout, taking into account both duration and intensity. A one-hour ride at exactly FTP = 100. Easy rides score lower; hard interval sessions score higher.

adherence.title: {name} — adherence
adherence.noTrend: Not enough data yet to chart the trend.
